title: Production and Consumption of Natural Gas, Iran 1965-1969 (million cubic meters)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank); underlying data from National Iranian Oil Company
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION. Source document: data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1971_cep_vol7_statistical_annex.pdf, Table 8.9, page 85 of the PDF. Rendered to PNG via pdftoppm -r 200 and visually read. No value interpolated, estimated, or fabricated.
notes: Splits gas production/consumption between the Iranian Oil Operating Companies (Consortium) and the National Iranian Oil Company's own Naft-e-Shah field. Dominated by flaring (81.6% of Consortium production still flared in 1969) rather than consumption -- domestic gas consumption (household/industrial) was still a small fraction of production at this stage, well before the National Iranian Gas Company's 1970s trunk-pipeline buildout. Useful baseline for later comparison against data/raw/iran-data-portal/energy-environment-tables/consumption_of_natural_gas_1991-2006.xlsx (which only starts in 1991) -- a ~20-year gap between this table and that one remains unfilled.
retrieved_by: claude (manual transcription with visual verification, pdftoppm -r 200 render + Read tool)
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
